macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Futures Edge Higher Ahead of Inflation Data and Fed Commentary
items.summary: US futures are slightly up as markets await key inflation figures and fresh comments from Federal Reserve officials. Oil prices extend gains amid ongoing supply concerns.

items.title: ECB Expected to Hike Rates by 50bps
items.summary: The European Central Bank is anticipated to raise rates by 50 basis points in its upcoming decision, with a press conference to follow. This comes alongside recent EU PPI data beating expectations at 3.4% MoM and 2.1% YoY.

items.title: Tech Stocks Drive Indices to New Highs Amid AI Build-Out
items.summary: S&P 500, Nasdaq, KOSPI, and Taiwan indices hit new highs led by semiconductor and AI-related manufacturers; strategy of 'long AI, short macro' prevails despite Middle East tensions.

items.title: Lavrov and Rubio Discuss Russia-US Relations and Bilateral Contacts
items.summary: Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov and US Secretary of State Rubio held talks on the international agenda, Russia-US relations, and future bilateral contacts, per Russian reports.

items.title: AMD Surges on Data Center Beat; Novo Nordisk Tweaks Outlook on Wegovy Sales
items.summary: AMD shares surge after data centers drive earnings beat; Novo Nordisk adjusts outlook as Wegovy sales exceed expectations; other movers include SMCI on guidance, GOOG's $200B Anthropic deal, and SSNLF hitting $1T valuation.

items.title: US MBA 30-Year Mortgage Rate Rises to 6.45%
items.summary: The US MBA 30-year mortgage rate increased to 6.45% as of May 1, signaling persistent pressure in housing finance amid economic data releases.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Above $80,000 Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin jumped above $80,000 while Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin traded flat, with 80,057 traders liquidated for $204.98 million in the past 24 hours and $277.5 million net outflows from spot Bitcoin ETFs.

items.title: Crypto Market Sees $281 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced $281 million in liquidations according to CoinAnk data, highlighting ongoing volatility.

items.title: Crypto Market Faces $216M in Liquidations with Balanced Longs and Shorts
items.summary: Over the past 24 hours, $216 million in liquidations occurred with longs at $103 million and shorts at $113 million; Bitcoin and Ethereum saw significant long liquidations per Coinglass data.

items.title: Coinbase Suffers Major Outage Hours After Q1 2026 Earnings Miss
items.summary: Coinbase experienced a prolonged trading disruption due to an AWS data center overheating, shortly after reporting Q1 revenue of $1.41 billion missing estimates and negative EPS of -$1.49 amid Bitcoin's slide.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Drops 2% to $2.62 Trillion as Equities Retreat
items.summary: The total crypto market capitalization fell around 2% over the past 24 hours to $2.62 trillion, driven by a flight to safety in global markets.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: IBM Releases New AI Operating Model; 64% of CEOs Now Comfortable Making Strategic Decisions Based on AI
items.summary: IBM announced a new AI operating model at its Think 2026 conference, with the IBM Institute for Business Value revealing that 64% of CEOs are now comfortable making major strategic decisions based on AI-generated input. The announcement highlights a shift toward cost-discipline in AI adoption as the technology becomes more trusted in enterprise settings.

items.title: Canvas Learning Platform Targeted in Major Cyberattack; ShinyHunters Claims Responsibility
items.summary: Canvas, used by nearly 9,000 schools and universities, experienced a significant cyberattack that disrupted access during final exams. Hacker group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility, stating they accessed billions of private messages and records, forcing schools to delay exams.

items.title: Silicon Valley Tech Giants Expanding Military AI Contracts; Defense Spending Drives Rapid Adoption
items.summary: Major tech companies including Palantir, Anduril, and Google are increasingly selling AI-powered weapons systems to militaries, with defense budgets driving rapid adoption of autonomous systems and AI-enabled battlefield tools. The trend reflects growing competition and escalating military-tech spending globally.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: New VR Games Released: Flatout 4 Total Insanity VR, Spymaster, Evil Inside VR
items.summary: This week's VR releases include Flatout 4: Total Insanity VR for PCVR, Spymaster for Meta Quest, and Evil Inside VR for Meta Quest and PSVR 2. Additional news covers Hot Dogs Horseshoes & Hand Grenades 2, Compass, and Memoreum discount for Quest and PCVR.

items.title: Path of Exile 2 Official Launch Date Announced Alongside Major Patch 0.5
items.summary: Path of Exile 2 reveals its 1.0 launch plans, featuring Spirit Walker, Martial Artist Monk, new Atlas tree, main questline, and Masters of the Atlas updates in the biggest patch yet.

items.title: Escape from Tarkov Updates: Icebreaker Event, Console and Mobile Versions, Halloween Zombies
items.summary: Icebreaker event launches this month, Battlestate Games plans console and mobile ports, potential OG Factory return, FSR 4.0, reworked reconnect, and big Halloween zombie event.

items.title: GTA 6 Pricing Details and LEGO Batman Previews Revealed
items.summary: Latest gaming news stream covers GTA 6 pricing information and previews for LEGO Batman, among other updates.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple settles Siri lawsuit for $250 million, covering all iPhone owners
items.summary: Apple has agreed to a $250 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit over Siri privacy issues, making every iPhone owner eligible for compensation.

items.title: Intel signs preliminary deal to produce chips for Apple
items.summary: Intel has reportedly reached a preliminary agreement to manufacture chips for Apple, marking a significant partnership reminiscent of past collaborations, boosting Intel's stock to an all-time high.

items.title: Nintendo raises Switch 2 prices amid chip crisis
items.summary: Nintendo is increasing prices for the Switch 2 due to ongoing chip shortages, despite selling 19.86 million units last year, with forecasts predicting a sales drop next year.

items.title: Google launches screenless Fitbit Air fitness tracker
items.summary: Google rebrands 'Google Health' as Fitbit and enters the screenless fitness tracker market with the new Fitbit Air device.

items.title: Canvas learning platform recovers after cyberattack impacting thousands of schools
items.summary: Instructure's Canvas system, used by nearly 9,000 schools worldwide, was disrupted by a cyberattack from ShinyHunters but is now back online for most users after locking out students and faculty.

items.title: Samsung advances Galaxy Tab S12+ toward launch
items.summary: The Samsung Galaxy Tab S12+ is now one step closer to its official launch, as confirmed by recent developments.

items.title: Verizon continues layoffs under CEO Schulman
items.summary: Verizon is laying off more employees as CEO Hans Vestberg hands out additional pink slips amid ongoing cost-cutting measures.

items.title: New Linux 'Dirty Frag' zero-day vulnerability grants root access on major distros
items.summary: A critical zero-day exploit called 'Dirty Frag' affects all major Linux distributions, allowing attackers to gain root privileges.
